title: oxytropis pumilio
intro: Oxytropis pumilio, also known as the dwarf locoweed, is a perennial plant native to mountainous regions of North America. It is a member of the legume family and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat various respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Oxytropis pumilio include relief from bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate skin irritations. In herbal preparations, Oxytropis pumilio is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
